frameworks.name: normalization model
frameworks.one_line: Each neuron's response divided by weighted pool sum.

frameworks.name: recurrent amplification
frameworks.one_line: Weak inputs amplified more than strong by recurrent circuit.

frameworks.name: coupled neural integrators
frameworks.one_line: Family of recurrent circuits implementing weighted normalization.

core_question: How does a recurrent circuit implement weighted normalization and reproduce V1 dynamics including gamma oscillations?
key_assumptions: Normalization in V1 operates via recurrent amplification
Each pool weight specifies one neuron's contribution to another's normalization
V1 dynamics (e.g., gamma oscillations) are linked to normalization
manipulations: Circuit architecture / coupling among neural integrators
key_findings: Coupled neural integrator circuits implement normalization via recurrent amplification
Arbitrary weighted normalization arises from the recurrent circuit family
Some circuit instantiations recapitulate key V1 dynamic observations including gamma oscillations
interpretations: Recurrent amplification provides a mechanistic account of weighted normalization
Complex V1 dynamics emerge naturally from the normalization circuit
